channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel's Netanyahu says Gaza ceasefire deal is not complete
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said late Wednesday that a ceasefire agreement with Hamas is still not complete and the final details are being worked out.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More than a week into what could be the costliest wildfires in U.S. history, weary and anxious Los Angeles-area residents were hoping Wednesday for a favorable shift in the winds and an end to the constant fear that their lives might be further upended. The blazes that began Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: For LA water issues, misinformation spreads nearly as fast as the wildfires
item.description: A billionaire couple was accused of withholding water that could help stop Los Angeles' massive wildfires. Democratic leadership was blamed for fire hydrants running dry and for an empty reservoir. Firefighters were criticized for allegedly using “women's handbags" to fight the fires.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mediators herald Gaza ceasefire and hostage deal. Israel says final details are in flux
item.description: DOHA, Qatar (AP) — Israel and Hamas have agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: NAJIB JOBAIN, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: American woman on Paris-to-Boston flight dies in midair
item.description: A passenger died on board during an Air France flight from Paris to Boston on Tuesday despite life-saving efforts performed by a doctor on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Puerto Rico asks Trump for help after Venezuela's Maduro threatens invasion
item.description: Puerto Rican voters have overwhelmingly chosen to keep their U.S. citizenship, granted in 1917 by an act of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Governor Braun eliminates DEI initiatives from state government
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS — Governor Mike Braun signed an executive order on Wednesday to remove DEI initiatives from the state government. The executive order was one of more than a dozen signed by the new governor just days after his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: 5 Chicagoland natives to perform with the 'The President’s Own' Marine Band at Trump's inauguration
item.description: CHICAGO — President-elect Donald Trump will be sworn into office on Monday and five Chicagoland natives will be there to honor the new commander-in-chief. Five Chicagoland natives will perform as members of “The President’s Own” United States Marine Band during the inauguration on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: US recovers $31 million in Social Security payments to dead people
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government clawed back more than $31 million in Social Security payments that improperly went to dead people, a recovery that one official said Wednesday was “just the tip of the iceberg.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disabled Los Angeles fire victim escaped flames in wheelchair
item.description: Galen Buckwalter, who is paralyzed from the waist down, said he watched as the glow of the wildfire raced down the San Gabriel Mountains toward their home in the foothill community of Sierra Madre.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Coors Light is temporarily changing its name: Here's why
item.description: This year, Coors Light is turning the case of the Mondays into a literal case, with limited-edition packaging and designs.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 more firebugs charged as LA's new tough-on-crime DA heats up fight against looters and arsonists
item.description: Two men have been charged with felony arson in Los Angeles in unrelated cases as police and firefighters combat flames and crime in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actor Bill Hader 'in shock' returning to Palisades Fire neighborhood
item.description: "It's just gone. Everything," "Barry" actor Bill Hader said as he returned to the Pacific Palisades area.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Smugglers transported Mexican cartel drugs to Indianapolis: DOJ
item.description: Thwarted by the DEA, 11 drug traffickers have now been sentenced for their role in the major drug ring.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flags ordered to fly at full-staff on Inauguration Day in multiple states
item.description: GOP governors of at least eight states ordered flags to be flown at full-staff on Inauguration Day, bucking tradition by raising flags before the end of a customary 30-day mourning period following the death of former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to address the nation days before Trump's inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday, days before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from snacks, medicine
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, a potential 2028 candidate, wants to find common ground with Trump
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer wants a fresh start with President-elect Donald Trump but also warns that she won't back down over tariffs that she believes would hurt the auto industry in her state.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA proposes sweeping limits on nicotine in cigarettes
item.description: It's an 11th-hour plan from the Biden administration that will take years to go into effect, if it happens at all.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Fire tornadoes a risk under California's extreme wildfire conditions
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wildfires latest: A final round of dangerous fire weather and dry conditions is in the forecast
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians were on edge as winds began picking up during a final round of dangerous fire weather forecast for the region where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip next week’s inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, Fox 5

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip the inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Man avoids prosecution for fighting back against migrant subway attack, fatally stabbing 1
item.description: A man who was asleep on a New York City subway train when a group tried to rob him will not face charges after he fatally stabbed one of them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US must not become complacent to a growing terrorism threat, a Counterterrorism Center official says
item.description: McLEAN, Va. (AP) — Brett Holmgren got woken up early on New Year's Day by alerts that a driver had plowed into a crowd of revelers in New Orleans. The rampage, which killed 14 people, was the deadliest attack on U.S. soil in years and was inspired by the Islamic State group.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAFSA forms to make October release after two-year struggle
item.description: The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) forms will be released on Oct. 1 this year, the Department of Education announced on Wednesday, hitting the normal deadline after two chaotic years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: A 'Particularly Dangerous Situation' is forecast for fire-scarred Los Angeles area
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians were on edge as a final round of dangerous fire weather was forecast for the region on Wednesday, along with a rare warning of a “Particularly Dangerous Situation” for an area near where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward moon in roundabout journey
item.description: In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon is ending its 'Try Before You Buy' option for Prime members
item.description: The service, which allowed Amazon's Prime members to try on select clothing items, shoes and accessories at home before completing a purchase, is set to wind down in the coming weeks. The last day eligible customers can place orders using the subscriber benefit is Jan. 31, the company confirmed.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to save TikTok videos before the potential ban goes into effect
item.description: The deadline for a potential TikTok ban in the U.S. is fast approaching and many of the app's users are already taking steps to prepare, even as its fate remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
